displayTitle: Hands On the Part: Contact Testing

beats.narration: In contact testing the probe sits directly on the part: just a thin couplant film and your steady hand. The workhorse of field inspection.

payload.title: CONTACT TESTING
payload.sub: Probe, couplant, part — and a steady hand.

beats.narration: Sound must physically cross into the steel, but a trapped air layer reflects nearly all of it back. Couplant fills that gap so energy crosses.

payload.heading: Couplant bridges the air gap
payload.note: AIR GAP = WALL · DRY SPOT = NO SIGNAL

beats.narration: With a continuous film and solid contact, pressing harder adds almost nothing. Consistency wins: probe flat, film even, passes overlapping.
One case, one side, minutes to set up. That's why contact owns field welds, corrosion thickness surveys, lamination checks, and bond testing.
Here's the rule: coupling consistency beats coupling force. A steady film, a flat probe, a patient hand — that's what keeps your readings repeatable.

payload.sub: Steady film, flat probe, patient hand.
